steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence sets up a contrast: the blank describes how the conjecture appeared to many mathematicians, yet it "eventually yielded" to a proof. The blank must mean something like "impossible to solve" or "insurmountable" — a word that makes the eventual solution surprising.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Unequivocal': means "clear and unambiguous" — doesn't describe difficulty or impossibility. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Ineluctable': means "impossible to avoid" or "inevitable" — the opposite of the needed sense; if the conjecture were inevitable, yielding wouldn't be surprising. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Irreproachable': means "beyond criticism" or "faultless" — describes moral character, not difficulty. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Insuperable': means "impossible to overcome" — precisely captures the contrast: the conjecture seemed insurmountable, yet it yielded. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
